extraction.one_line_summary: WOODS Michael won Stage 4 by refusing to pull THOMAS Geraint after bridging at 6km, then outsprint him at 100m while Thomas crashed post-finish.
tactical_patterns: Woods deliberately refused pulls after Thomas bridged at 6km, citing time trial disadvantage as justification for wheelsucking.
Thomas bridged to Woods at 6km despite having time trial advantage, revealing overconfidence or team pressure to gain stage time.
O'Connor shadowed the Thomas-Woods duel to capture podium seconds without expending energy in their tactical stalemate.

rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: executed perfect psychological ploy by verbalizing TT weakness to justify not working, then had legs to outsprint anyway
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: crashed post-finish indicating complete redlining, lost 21 seconds and yellow despite bridging successfully at 6km
rider_signals.PATILLI Simone: negative: breakaway companion Cort attacked and Patilli cracked when considered countering, got absorbed by peloton on final climb
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: finished second on stage without leading Thomas-Woods group, maximizing GC position at 21 seconds through parasitic tactics
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: race leader lost 33 seconds and yellow jersey, completely dropped on final climb without teammate support visible
